source: Bank of Canada, Business Outlook Survey (Valet API, public)
caveat: Because the responses are presented here as four-quarter moving averages, these charts may not show the same movements as the aggregate BOS data published each quarter.

indicators.name: Past sales growth
indicators.definition: Over the past 12 months, the rate of increase in your firm’s sales volume (compared with the previous 12 months) was…

subcomponents.definition: Over the past 12 months, the rate of increase in your firm’s sales volume (compared with the previous 12 months) was…

indicators.name: Past sales declines
indicators.definition: Over the past 12 months, did your firm experience a decline in the level of sales? Shown as the share of firms reporting a decline.

subcomponents.name: Share of firms reporting declines
subcomponents.definition: Over the past 12 months, did your firm experience a decline in the level of sales? Shown as the share of firms reporting a decline.

indicators.name: Future sales growth
indicators.definition: Over the next 12 months, the rate of increase in your firm’s sales volume (compared with the past 12 months) is expected to be…

subcomponents.name: Future sales (balance of opinion)

subcomponents.name: Indicators of future sales
subcomponents.definition: Compared with 12 months ago, have your recent indicators (order books, advanced bookings, sales inquiries, etc.)…

indicators.name: Wages
indicators.definition: Over the next 12 months, are increases in labour costs expected to be higher, lower or about the same rate as over the past 12 months?

subcomponents.definition: Over the next 12 months, are increases in labour costs expected to be higher, lower or about the same rate as over the past 12 months?

indicators.definition: Over the past 3 months, how have the terms and conditions for obtaining financing changed (compared with the previous 3 months)?

indicators.name: Regional BOS indicator
indicators.definition: Each region's contribution to a standardized regional Business Outlook Survey indicator (different scale and a shorter history than the by-region balance-of-opinion series).

subcomponents.name: Contribution to regional indicator
subcomponents.definition: Each region's contribution to a standardized regional Business Outlook Survey indicator (different scale and a shorter history than the by-region balance-of-opinion series).

indicators.name: Business sentiment — Canada vs U.S.
indicators.definition: Small-business confidence. Canada: CFIB Business Barometer (0–100, 50 = neutral). United States: NFIB Small Business Optimism Index (index, 1986 = 100). The two use different scales, so single-country views use each series' own axis and the US–Canada view normalizes both to index 100.
indicators.source: Trading Economics — CFIB Business Barometer (Canada) & NFIB Small Business Optimism Index (U.S.)

series.label: United States
series.source: NFIB Small Business Optimism Index (via Trading Economics)
